categories.name: Agent SDK / framework
categories.description: Code-first libraries and frameworks for defining agents, tools, prompts, model calls, and shared state in custom agent applications.

categories.name: Agent graph / runtime
categories.description: Execution engines for graph-based or durable agent workflows, including checkpointing, resumability, human-in-the-loop steps, and long-running state management.

categories.name: Agent platform / control plane
categories.description: Production platforms and control planes for deploying, running, managing, and governing agent systems across self-hosted, managed, or hybrid environments.

categories.name: RAG / knowledge system
categories.description: Retrieval-centered systems for ingesting documents, building indexes, searching knowledge sources, and grounding agent responses.

categories.name: Memory / context system
categories.description: Memory and context layers that persist user state, conversation history, profiles, or knowledge graphs across agent steps and sessions.

categories.name: Low-code builder / agent app
categories.description: Visual builders and packaged applications for assembling agents, chat or RAG workflows, automations, and assistants with less custom code.

categories.name: Tool / protocol / connector layer
categories.description: Protocols, tool registries, connector catalogs, and auth brokers that let agents discover, call, and coordinate external systems.

categories.name: Developer / computer-use agent
categories.description: Agents and support tools built for software development, browser automation, desktop control, terminal workflows, and other computer-use tasks.

categories.name: Data / ingestion / MLOps substrate
categories.description: Data and ML infrastructure for collecting, parsing, transforming, labeling, evaluating, or orchestrating the pipelines that feed agent systems.

categories.name: Observability / evals
categories.description: Tracing, evaluation, monitoring, audit, and incident-analysis tools for understanding agent behavior, quality, cost, safety, and production reliability.

categories.name: Model serving / gateway
categories.description: Inference servers, local model shells, provider gateways, routers, and API proxies that expose models behind a consistent serving layer.

categories.name: Database / infrastructure product
categories.description: Databases, vector or graph stores, memory substrates, deployment platforms, and other infrastructure that agent systems rely on at runtime.

categories.name: Protocol / standard only
categories.description: Specifications and standards that define how agent systems communicate or interoperate, without being full product implementations themselves.

frameworks.name: AG2
frameworks.description: Community continuation of Microsoft's AutoGen multi-agent framework.

frameworks.name: Agency Swarm
frameworks.description: Open-source framework for building structured, reliable AI agent networks where each agent has clearly defined roles and communication channels.

frameworks.name: Agent Zero
frameworks.description: Open, dynamic agent framework packaged as a Dockerized Linux system with a desktop, tool use, code execution, memory, and an extensible skill hub.

frameworks.name: AgentDock
frameworks.description: AgentDock is a general-purpose Build Anything with AI Agents framework.

frameworks.name: Agentica
frameworks.description: Agentica uses TypeScript compiler analysis to automatically generate type-safe function schemas for LLM tool calling, eliminating manual schema definition.

frameworks.name: Agently
frameworks.description: AI application runtime for structured outputs, observable actions, runtime skills, MCP capabilities, process streams, and recoverable workflows.

frameworks.name: AgentScope
frameworks.description: Production-ready agent framework with an event bus, human-in-the-loop hooks, permission controls, and multi-tenant session serving.

frameworks.name: AgentScope Java
frameworks.description: Java port of Alibaba's AgentScope framework for building LLM applications using an agent-oriented programming model.

frameworks.name: agentUniverse
frameworks.description: AgentUniverse is a Python LLM multi-agent framework for building collaborative agent applications.

frameworks.name: Atomic Agents
frameworks.description: Lightweight modular agent framework built on Pydantic.

frameworks.name: AutoAgents
frameworks.description: AutoAgents is a Rust multi-agent framework from liquidos-ai for building, deploying, and coordinating multiple intelligent agents.

frameworks.name: AutoGen
frameworks.description: Microsoft's foundational multi-agent conversation framework.

frameworks.name: BabyAGI
frameworks.description: Experimental self-building agent framework centered on a function database, dependency graph, secrets handling, and dashboard-managed execution.

frameworks.name: BeeAI Framework
frameworks.description: BeeAI Framework is an open-source (Apache 2.0) framework for building production-ready multi-agent systems, with first-class parity across Python and TypeScript.

frameworks.name: BotSharp
frameworks.description: C#/.NET framework for building AI bot and agent applications with NLU, vision, audio processing, and controllable conversation pipelines.

frameworks.name: Boxcars
frameworks.description: Ruby AI framework focused on composable boxcars (chains/tools) with LLM backends.

frameworks.name: CAMEL
frameworks.description: Multi-agent research framework studying collaborative AI systems at scale.

frameworks.name: Cheshire Cat AI
frameworks.description: Cheshire Cat provides a production-ready, plugin-based AI agent server with memory, tool use, and RAG over personal/organizational documents.

frameworks.name: Context7
frameworks.description: Context7 is an MCP server that fetches live, up-to-date documentation for libraries and injects it directly into LLM/AI coding agent contexts.

frameworks.name: CopilotKit
frameworks.description: Frontend SDK for building agent-native UI in React.

frameworks.name: CrewAI
frameworks.description: Python framework for production multi-agent automation, combining role-based Crews with event-driven Flows for tighter workflow control.

frameworks.name: Crush
frameworks.description: Terminal coding agent from Charmbracelet that connects local tools, code, workflows, and multiple LLM providers in a persistent session.

frameworks.name: Deep Agents
frameworks.description: Deep Agents is LangChain's batteries-included agent harness.

frameworks.name: DeepCode
frameworks.description: DeepCode is an open agentic coding research harness from HKUDS (Hong Kong University) supporting Paper2Code, Text2Web, and Text2Backend workflows.

frameworks.name: DSPy
frameworks.description: Framework for programming language models with modular components and optimizers for prompts and weights across RAG, agents, and classifiers.

frameworks.name: Eino
frameworks.description: Eino is ByteDance's production Go-based LLM application framework with composable retrieval, memory, and orchestration abstractions.

frameworks.name: Eliza
frameworks.description: Multi-agent simulation framework for autonomous AI agents, originally popularized by ai16z.

frameworks.name: Embabel
frameworks.description: JVM agent framework for authoring goal-directed flows that mix LLM prompts, code, domain models, and intelligent path finding.

frameworks.name: EvoAgentX
frameworks.description: Self-evolving multi-agent ecosystem that auto-generates and refines agent pipelines.

frameworks.name: Firebase Genkit
frameworks.description: Genkit is Google's production-backed open-source AI app framework for JS/Go/Python.

frameworks.name: Forge
frameworks.description: Python reliability layer for self-hosted LLM tool-calling loops with optional workflow structure, retries, validation, and parsing recovery.

frameworks.name: GenAIScript
frameworks.description: Microsoft's automatable GenAI scripting environment.

frameworks.name: GenericAgent
frameworks.description: GenericAgent is a minimal (~3K lines), self-evolving autonomous agent framework.

frameworks.name: GenSX
frameworks.description: GenSX is a TypeScript framework for building agents and workflows using React-like component composition.

frameworks.name: Google ADK Go
frameworks.description: Google ADK Go is the Go-language member of the Google Agent Development Kit family, extending the same agent abstractions to Go developers.

frameworks.name: Google ADK (Java)
frameworks.description: Provides code-first agent building for Java developers with support for Gemini models, tool calling, multi-agent orchestration, and memory/session persistence.

frameworks.name: Google ADK (JavaScript)
frameworks.description: Open-source TypeScript Agent Development Kit for building, evaluating, and deploying AI agents with Google's ADK abstractions.

frameworks.name: Google Agent Development Kit (ADK)
frameworks.description: Google's open-source agent development framework, tightly integrated with Gemini models and Google Cloud infrastructure.

frameworks.name: Google Antigravity SDK
frameworks.description: Google Antigravity SDK is an official Python SDK for building Antigravity/Gemini-powered agents.

frameworks.name: Gorilla
frameworks.description: LLM function call training, evaluation, and inference framework from UC Berkeley.

frameworks.name: Griptape
frameworks.description: Modular Python agent framework with strict separation of concerns.

frameworks.name: Guidance
frameworks.description: Pythonic programming interface for steering language models with constrained generation, structured outputs, tool use, loops, and conditionals.

frameworks.name: Instructor
frameworks.description: Python library for structured LLM output with Pydantic validation, retries, and tool-call friendly schemas.

frameworks.name: IntentKit
frameworks.description: Agent framework for building specialized skill-based AI agents.

frameworks.name: JARVIS (HuggingGPT)
frameworks.description: Microsoft Research system for connecting LLM controllers to specialist ML models and tools from the broader AI community.

frameworks.name: JoyAgent-JDGenie
frameworks.description: Production-grade universal multi-agent framework from JD.com.

frameworks.name: KaibanJS
frameworks.description: JavaScript-native multi-agent framework that applies Kanban-style workflow management to AI agent orchestration.

frameworks.name: LangChain
frameworks.description: Framework for building agents and LLM applications by composing interoperable components, integrations, tools, and model calls.

frameworks.name: LangChain Elixir
frameworks.description: Elixir library for integrating AI services and self-hosted models into applications using LangChain-style chat, tool, and model abstractions.

frameworks.name: LangChain Go
frameworks.description: Go implementation of LangChain for composing LLM-powered applications with model, chain, tool, vector store, and memory abstractions.

frameworks.name: LangChain.js
frameworks.description: The official TypeScript/JavaScript port of LangChain.

frameworks.name: Langchain.rb
frameworks.description: Ruby port of LangChain with vectorstore, memory, and tool abstractions.

frameworks.name: LangChain4j
frameworks.description: Idiomatic Java library for building JVM LLM applications with unified provider APIs, vector stores, RAG, tools, agents, and Spring or Quarkus integration.

frameworks.name: Langroid
frameworks.description: Multi-agent LLM framework with a task-delegation architecture.

frameworks.name: Laravel AI
frameworks.description: The Laravel AI SDK provides a unified, expressive API for interacting with AI providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini).

frameworks.name: LazyLLM
frameworks.description: Chinese multi-agent framework with RAG pipeline support, easy tool integration, and model fine-tuning capabilities.

frameworks.name: llama-cpp-agent
frameworks.description: Unmaintained Python framework for llama.cpp-based chat, function calling, structured output, RAG, and tool-using agentic chains.

frameworks.name: LLPhant
frameworks.description: PHP-native generative AI framework with LangChain-inspired abstractions for RAG, memory, and tool use.

frameworks.name: Magentic
frameworks.description: Python decorator library for turning prompts into typed functions with structured outputs, streaming, tool calls, and LLM-assisted retries.

frameworks.name: Marvin
frameworks.description: Python framework for producing structured outputs and building observable agentic workflows with tasks delegated to LLMs.

frameworks.name: Mastra
frameworks.description: TypeScript-first AI application framework from the team behind Gatsby.

frameworks.name: MetaGPT
frameworks.description: Opinionated multi-agent software engineering harness with pre-built roles (ProductManager/Engineer/Architect).

frameworks.name: Microsoft Agent Framework
frameworks.description: Microsoft framework for building, orchestrating, and deploying production AI agents and multi-agent workflows in .NET and Python.

frameworks.name: Mirascope
frameworks.description: Minimalist Python anti-framework for LLM interactions that favors composable primitives over heavy abstractions.

frameworks.name: MS-Agent
frameworks.description: MS-Agent is ModelScope's lightweight framework for agentic execution.

frameworks.name: NeMo Guardrails
frameworks.description: NVIDIA toolkit for adding programmable guardrails to LLM applications, including dialog paths, output controls, and structured extraction.

frameworks.name: Open Agents
frameworks.description: Open source template/harness for building cloud agents from Vercel Labs.

frameworks.name: Open Multi-Agent
frameworks.description: TypeScript-native multi-agent orchestration framework.

frameworks.name: OpenAI Agents SDK
frameworks.description: OpenAI's official production multi-agent orchestration framework and successor to OpenAI Swarm.

frameworks.name: OpenAI Swarm
frameworks.description: Swarm is OpenAI's lightweight experimental multi-agent orchestration framework with minimal built-in persistence.

frameworks.name: Ouroboros
frameworks.description: Local-first runtime for replayable, specification-driven AI coding workflows across Claude Code, Codex CLI, OpenCode, Gemini, and MCP.

frameworks.name: OWL (CAMEL-AI)
frameworks.description: Opinionated, batteries-included multi-agent application from CAMEL-AI for real-world task automation.

frameworks.name: page-agent

frameworks.name: PocketFlow

frameworks.name: Promptise Foundry
frameworks.description: Promptise Foundry (the foundation layer for agentic intelligence) is a batteries-included async Python framework.

frameworks.name: Qwen-Agent

frameworks.name: Rasa

frameworks.name: Rig

frameworks.name: Rowboat

frameworks.name: smolagents

frameworks.name: Spring AI Alibaba

frameworks.name: Stakpak Agent

frameworks.name: SuperAGI

frameworks.name: TradingAgents

frameworks.name: TypedAI
frameworks.description: TypeScript-first platform for autonomous agents and LLM workflows, including chat, function calling, observability, and coding-agent features.

frameworks.name: Upsonic
frameworks.description: Python agent framework purpose-built for enterprise/fintech use cases with a client-server architecture.

frameworks.name: Vercel AI SDK
frameworks.description: The Vercel AI SDK is a widely-adopted TypeScript toolkit for building AI-powered Next.js and Node.js applications.

frameworks.name: VoltAgent
frameworks.description: TypeScript-first AI agent engineering platform with observability-first design and visual debugging console.

frameworks.name: AgentFS

frameworks.name: Agentlas OS Agent Operation Environment (AOE)

frameworks.name: AIOS

frameworks.name: Astron Agent
frameworks.description: Astron Agent is iFlyTek's enterprise agentic platform, a major player in the Chinese enterprise AI market.

frameworks.name: Auto Deep Researcher 24x7

frameworks.name: AX (Agent Executor)

frameworks.name: Back4app
frameworks.description: Back4app is a managed backend-as-a-service (BaaS) platform built on top of the open-source Parse Platform (parse-community/parse-server).

frameworks.name: Bindu

frameworks.name: Burr

frameworks.name: Chidori

frameworks.name: Claude Agent SDK

frameworks.name: Conductor
frameworks.description: Open-source workflow orchestration engine originally built at Netflix.

frameworks.name: ControlFlow
frameworks.description: Workflow state rides on Prefect's own runtime.

frameworks.name: Convex
frameworks.description: Convex is the open-source reactive database for app developers, now positioned as the backend building blocks for your agents.

frameworks.name: CUA (Computer-Use Agent)

frameworks.name: CubeSandbox

frameworks.name: Dapr

frameworks.name: dimos

frameworks.name: Dora
frameworks.description: Dora (Dataflow-Oriented Robotic Architecture) is middleware for building AI-based robotic applications.

frameworks.name: E2B

frameworks.name: Eve
frameworks.description: Eve (The Framework for Building Agents) is Vercel's filesystem-first framework for durable AI agents, in public beta as of June 2026.

frameworks.name: Gastown
frameworks.description: Each agent has its own context window and state; Gastown manages routing, scheduling, and inter-agent communication.

frameworks.name: Hatchet
frameworks.description: Orchestration platform for background tasks, AI agents, and durable workflows with queues, retries, monitoring, alerting, and logs.

frameworks.name: HelixDB

frameworks.name: holaOS
frameworks.description: Local-first desktop agent for work context, persistent memory, integrations, MCP tool use, and proactive workflows across daily applications.

frameworks.name: InsForge
frameworks.description: Open-source backend platform for agentic coding.

frameworks.name: LangGraph
frameworks.description: Durable execution runtime from the LangChain team; the primary way LangChain users build stateful, multi-step agents.

frameworks.name: Letta
frameworks.description: Stateful agent execution runtime (formerly MemGPT) with persistent, editable memory architecture.

frameworks.name: Memobase
frameworks.description: User-profile memory system for LLM applications that stores long-term user context for companions, education tools, and personalized assistants.

frameworks.name: Microsoft Promptflow
frameworks.description: Microsoft's LLM app development toolkit for building, evaluating, and deploying prompt flows.

frameworks.name: Moltis
frameworks.description: Moltis is a local-first persistent agent server in Rust.

frameworks.name: NanoClaw

frameworks.name: NVIDIA OpenShell

frameworks.name: Omnigent

frameworks.name: OpenFang

frameworks.name: OpenViking

frameworks.name: Pulumi

frameworks.name: Helix

frameworks.name: LangSmith

frameworks.description: Open-source AI engineering platform for tracing, evaluating, deploying, and monitoring LLM and agent applications.

frameworks.name: Cognee

frameworks.name: Semantic Router
frameworks.description: Semantic Router performs fast semantic intent classification using vector similarity over a route index and supports pluggable index backends.

frameworks.name: SGLang
frameworks.description: High-performance serving framework for large language and multimodal models, including fast inference, structured generation, and new model support.

frameworks.name: vLLM
frameworks.description: High-throughput and memory-efficient LLM inference engine from UC Berkeley.

frameworks.name: Deep Lake
frameworks.description: Database for AI applications with a deep-learning-optimized storage format for storing, searching, and training over data plus vectors.

frameworks.name: HydraDB
frameworks.description: HydraDB provides data or infrastructure primitives for agent systems.

frameworks.name: Agent Client Protocol (ACP)
frameworks.description: Protocol that standardizes communication between code editors and coding agents, with schemas and Rust artifacts for wire messages.

frameworks.name: Model Context Protocol (MCP)
frameworks.description: Open protocol specification for connecting LLM applications to external tools, data sources, and context providers.

related_lists.name: Awesome AI Infrastructure
related_lists.description: Curated resources for AI infrastructure, platforms, and operational tooling.

related_lists.name: Awesome Lists
related_lists.description: A collection of curated resource lists.
